stories.title: Operation Save Orbán: Trump deploys Vance to Hungary
stories.summary: The Trump administration is going all in to save its No. 1 ally in Europe. U.S. Vice President JD Vance is set to land in Budapest on Tuesday for a high-stakes intervention that underscores how far the White House is willing to go to shore up Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán before the April 12 national election. Orbán is flailing in the polls , as anti-corruption opposition candidate Péter Magyar surges ahead in his bid to claim power in Budapest after 16 years of leadership by the ruling

stories.title: ‘Who doesn’t enjoy a good catfight story?’: Kallas shrugs off von der Leyen tensions
stories.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas played down talk of a power struggle with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, after POLITICO reported in March that the two were on a “collision course” over who should steer the bloc’s response to global crises. The report — citing EU diplomats, officials and lawmakers — pointed to an “evident” rivalry between the two, fueled by overlapping responsibilities and competing centers of power in Brussels. “Who doesn’t enjoy a good catfight story?”

stories.title: Costa Rica strikes deal to accept third country deportees from US
stories.summary: Central American country to receive up to 25 migrants a week expelled as part of Trump’s immigration crackdown Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The Costa Rican government has agreed to receive up to 25 deported migrants a week from the United States, the latest deal in the Trump administration’s unprecedented efforts to deport scores of people to “third countries”. With the new agreement, Costa Rica seeks a closer alliance with Donald Trump’s governme

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy offers expertise on keeping waterways open amid Middle East conflict
stories.summary: ‘Ukraine has expertise concerning sea waterways, and the defence and reopening of maritime traffic,’ says president. What we know on day 1,500 Volodymyr Zelenskyy offered on Thursday to provide Ukraine’s expertise in dealing with freedom of navigation in the Black Sea to those countries considering how to keep the strait of Hormuz open amid the conflict in the Middle East. The Ukraine president, speaking in his nightly video address, said the foreign minister, Andrii Sybiha, had taken part in a

stories.title: Iran war puts Cuba on the back burner
stories.summary: Regime change in Cuba has long been on President Donald Trump’s to-do list. But with Tehran dominating the agenda, Havana is getting a reprieve — for now. Given the amount of resources and attention Iran is consuming, and the hassle a face off with Russia would cause, Trump and his aides decided it was worth letting Cuba’s regime have a longer lifeline for now, said a person familiar with the administration’s conversations on Cuba. But the administration still intends to change the Cuban politic

stories.title: It’s time to reinvent NATO, says Ukraine’s envoy to the alliance
stories.summary: KYIV — While NATO is teetering , Ukraine is watching on with astonishment — and a grain of hope. U.S. President Donald Trump this week said he was seriously considering withdrawing his country from the alliance. The statement alarmed NATO members like Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk, who said the scenario “looks like Putin’s dream plan.” But Alyona Getmanchuk, Ukraine’s ambassador to NATO, reacted to the news with surprising optimism, telling POLITICO the current turbul

stories.title: Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win?
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán, an icon for the global far right, could face defeat despite an electoral system weighted in his favour Hungarians go to the polls on 12 April in Europe’s most consequential election of the year, with Viktor Orbán, the country’s illiberal prime minister and global far-right icon, facing possible defeat, after 16 years in power, by a former loyalist, Péter Magyar. Continue reading...

stories.title: US jobs market surpassed expectations in March but February losses were worse than first reported
stories.summary: Employers added 178,000 new jobs in March and unemployment rate fell to 4.3%, ahead of economists’ predictions Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US labor market picked up in March as employers showed signs of resilience amid the US-Israel war in Iran . After an extraordinary contraction in February , employers added 178,000 jobs last month, ahead of economists’ expectations of about 70,000. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Letting the algorithm rip’: no legal basis for lack of human override of aged care funding tool, inquiry hears
stories.summary: Department says it’s received 834 requests for a review of tool’s assessments since it launched in November Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast There appears to be no legal barrier for a human to override a controversial algorithm that determines financial support for elderly Australians, a Senate inquiry has heard, despite government assessors being banned from doing so. The Integrated Assessment Tool (IAT), introduced in November as part of aged care Support at Home re

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition as key election nears
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: Troops see that the defense minister is not up to the task, and they are hoping for change – the former face of the Hungarian military speaks out
stories.summary: The former face of the Hungarian Armed Forces, who graduated from the British military academy alongside PM Orbán's son, gave an interview to Telex. He described the tensions the defence minister's decisions created among the troops. He was there when Hungarian soldiers were sent out of NATO meetings, and the PM's son shared sensitive details about the planned mission to Chad with him. A written summary of our video interview.

stories.title: ‘A wow moment’: ancient Romanian gold helmet returned in plea deal with theft suspects
stories.summary: Prosecutors unveil artefact linked to lost Dacian civilisation after it was stolen from Dutch museum last year A priceless ancient gold helmet from Romania that was stolen last year from a museum in the Netherlands, has been recovered as part of a plea deal reached with the suspects. Under the guard of balaclava-wearing police, prosecutors unveiled the 2,500-year-old Coțofenești helmet, which is considered a cultural icon of Romania, during a news conference on Thursday in the eastern Dutch city

stories.title: Az iráni háború minden egyes nappal egyre komolyabb károkat okoz Magyarországnak
stories.summary: Ha negatívba fordul a külkereskedelmi mérlegünk, nem sok jóra számíthatunk a hitelminősítőknél.

stories.title: A magyarok már nem tartanak attól, hogy belesodródhatunk az orosz–ukrán háborúba
stories.summary: Milyen erős a háborús félelem a társadalomban, vizsgálta a választási kampány utolsó szakaszában a Policy Solutions. 2022-ben döntőnek bizonyult a háborús fenyegetés, és még 2025 őszén is 46 százaléknyian tartották reális veszélynek, hogy Magyarország háborúba sodródhat. Most már a magyarok 62 százaléka nem tart ettől. Felmérésük szerint a többség a Tisza győzelmét várja, és tőlük várják a gazdaság fellendülését is.

stories.title: A Tisza ellen dolgozó „Henry” csapatának lehetett köze a párt irodájában talált kémtollhoz is
stories.summary: Magyar Pétert kevesen vették komolyan, amikor másfél éve bemutatta a kémtollnak nevezett eszközt a nyilvánosságnak. Pedig a Direkt36 birtokába került dokumentumok szerint ehhez is közük lehetett a párt bedöntését célzó titkos művelet irányítóinak.

stories.title: „Mindent el fognak követni most ellene” – mondta Ruszin-Szendi a Telexnek nyilatkozó századosról
stories.summary: A volt vezérkari főnök szerint Pálinkás Szilveszternek tisztelet jár, mert Magyarország érdeke, hogy a Honvédség megszabaduljon a politikai elnyomástól.

stories.title: „Irányíthat-e olyan, akinek nincs hova hazamennie?” – kérdezték Orbánt a húsvéti interjújában Magyarról
stories.summary: De a miniszterelnök azt állította, ő nem akart ebben a választási kampányban azzal foglalkozni, hogy milyen ember az ő kihívója.

stories.title: Rekordot jelentő, 90 ezer magyar szavazhat a külképviseleteken
stories.summary: Az utolsó napon még csaknem 3700-an jelentkeztek a külképviseleti névjegyzékbe.

stories.title: Pálinkás Szilveszter: A Honvédelmi Minisztérium és a katonai vezetés kiszolgálja a miniszterelnök fiának az akaratát, bármekkora hülyeség is
stories.summary: A leszerelési szándékát már jelző százados szerint a csádi missziót azért erőlteti a kormány, mert Orbán Gáspár ragaszkodik hozzá. Pedig nemzeti érdek nem fűződik az afrikai országban tervezett magyar katonai jelenléthez, amelynél a kormányfő fia is nagy veszteséggel kalkulált. Egyáltalán, hogy került képbe Csád a Honvédségnél?

stories.title: Húsvétra ingyenesen elérhetővé teszik a készítők a Magyar Péter-filmet
stories.summary: Azt írták, inkább a társadalmi hatást helyezik előtérbe a hagyományos forgalmazási és üzleti szempontokkal szemben.

stories.title: Ausztria kitiltotta az amerikai katonai gépeket a légteréből
stories.summary: Az alkancellár szerint az osztrákok nem akarnak részt venni Trump káoszpolitikájában és háborújában.

stories.title: Két szavazólap egy névre, halottnak küldött boríték – olvasók jeleztek rendellenességeket a levélszavazásról
stories.summary: Továbbra is sok furcsasághoz vezet, hogy nincsenek összhangban a magyar és román adatbázisok.

stories.title: Közös kampányeseményt tart Orbán Viktor és az amerikai alelnök kedden Budapesten
stories.summary: Előtte Orbán Viktor a Karmelitában fogadja J. D. Vance-t, akivel közös sajtótájékoztatót is tartanak.

stories.title: Sakkfeladvány: két bástya, két lépés és egy elégedett pillanat
stories.summary: A figurák egy sorban állnak, könnyen áttekinthető állás, elegáns befejezéssel.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor nullával osztott
stories.summary: Orbánt annyira letekerte a Meta, hogy attól egészen megtáltosodott, de akármennyit is ukránozik, Magyar Péter így is több utcahosszal veri a Facebookon. Egymillió fiktív guruló dollár nyomára bukkantunk a Tiszánál, miközben már megint megdőlt minden rekord a Lájkbajnokságban.

stories.title: Egy nap alatt már egymilliónál is többen látták a Pálinkás Szilveszter századossal készült interjút a katonák valóságáról
stories.summary: A 33 éves katona, aki korábban a Magyar Honvédség országos toborzókampányának arca volt, csatornaszagról, penészes falakról, elavult eszközökről és Orbán Gáspárról is beszélt a Telexnek.

stories.title: Több tucat fütyülő, sípoló tüntető fogadta Orbánt Szombathelyen
stories.summary: Ezt a miniszterelnök is észrevette, már-már többet üzengetett az ellenzéki tüntetőknek, mint saját szimpatizánsainak.

stories.title: Beütött a tavaszi szünet, tinik árasztották el Magyar Péter országjárását
stories.summary: A Tisza elnöke ki is használta ezt, az átlagosnál többet beszélt a fiatalokhoz. Gyártsanak tartalmat az internetre, ahhoz úgyis jobban értenek a digitális harcosoknál, kérte tőlük. Magyar Péter csütörtökön Algyőn is járt, és Mórahalmon, ahol azt kérdezte a polgármestertől, hogy jobb-e aranybudin ülni, mint a sima fehéren.

stories.title: Magyar Péter hősnek nevezte Pálinkás Szilvesztert, a Telexnek kitálaló honvédségi századost
stories.summary: Szerinte botrány és szégyen, ahol „tart ez a hatalom”.

stories.title: Kedden érkezik Magyarországra J. D. Vance amerikai alelnök
stories.summary: Húsvét után érkezik az Egyesült Államok második legfontosabb politikusa, és másnapig marad.

stories.title: Orbán megmondta Hajdú Péternek, hogy miért nincsen még börtönben Matolcsy György
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök kapott kérdést a G-napról és Antall József utolsó telefonjáról is, de érdemi választ keveset adott. Szerinte Ukrajna nemcsak egy szenvedő ország, hanem egy terrorista állam is.

stories.title: Kanye West elfelejtette, hogyan kell zenét csinálni
stories.summary: A botrányok majd bocsánatkérései után a részben rehabilitált Ye új albummal tért vissza. A Bully című lemezen minden ott van, amitől a korábbi anyagai jók voltak, mégis összecsapott lett, amin a mesterséges intelligencia használata inkább csak javít.

stories.title: Szijjártó szerint nem volt kellő jogi alap az orosz oligarcha nővérének szankcionálása mögött, uniós ítélet mondta ki az ellenkezőjét
stories.summary: Szijjártó a jogi alap hiányával magyarázta Gulbahor Iszmailova leszedését az EU-s szankciós listáról, akinek ügyében Lavrov felhívta, miután a magyar külügyminiszter is jóváhagyta a felvételét. Számos más, Szijjártó ügyködésével hírbe hozott oligarcha mögött is bukott perek vannak.

stories.title: Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf honvédelmi miniszter reagált a Honvédség állapotáról kitálaló századosra
stories.summary: Sajnálatosnak tartja, hogy egyes személyek politikai célok szolgálatába állítják a pártpolitikától független Magyar Honvédséget.

stories.title: Macron lauds Europe's 'predictability' on visit to Japan in apparent swipe at Trump
stories.summary: Macron's office had expressed surprise at Trump's criticism of France for not letting planes carrying military supplies for the conflict fly over its territory.

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: Is the US committing war crimes by targeting Iran’s civilian infrastructure?
stories.summary: International law experts ‘seriously concerned’ about ‘strikes on schools, health centres and homes’ in contravention of Geneva conventions Donald Trump, other senior US officials and their cheerleaders appear to be embracing attacks – and threats of attacks – on Iranian civilian infrastructure, which legal experts say appears to constitute serious war crimes under international law. In his rambling national address on Wednesday, the US president warned that if Iran did not reach an unspecified

stories.title: Officials warn US is running out of targets to strike in Iran
stories.summary: The Pentagon is running out of strategically important targets to hit in Iran, even as President Donald Trump insists the U.S.  will keep pummeling  Tehran for up to three weeks. The new timeline — which Trump announced Wednesday in an address to the nation — leaves few options for conducting the kind of air strikes necessary to eliminate the hidden ballistic missile stockpiles he seeks to destroy, according to two current defense officials and a former Trump administration official. T

stories.title: ‘Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!’ Trump threatens Iran’s civilian infrastructure
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump delivered a new threat to Iran late Thursday evening, warning that the American military would strike civilian infrastructure including bridges and power plants. “Our Military, the greatest and most powerful (by far!) anywhere in the World, hasn’t even started destroying what’s left in Iran,” Trump said on Truth Social . “Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants! New Regime leadership knows what has to be done, and has to be done, FAST!” The Pe

stories.title: Sweden boards suspected shadow fleet tanker linked to oil spill
stories.summary: Swedish authorities on Friday boarded an oil tanker on the EU sanctions list off the country’s southern coast after detecting a possible oil spill. Authorities began tracking the Flora 1, a vessel flying the Cameroonian flag, after detecting a 12-kilometer-long oil slick east of the island of Gotland in the central Baltic Sea on Thursday morning. After escorting the ship to the Swedish port of Ystad, police and coast guard officers boarded the ship for inspection on Friday as part of a prelimina

stories.title: Trump tariff tweaks smooth the way for EU-US trade deal approval
stories.summary: A trade truce between the European Union and the U.S. looks more likely after U.S. President Donald Trump lowered the level of some of the most contentious tariffs on Thursday. On the one-year anniversary of the so-called “Liberation Day” tariff package — when Trump rolled out reciprocal tariffs on imports from countries his administration identified as having unfair trade surpluses with the U.S. — the White House announced changes to duties on steel and pharmaceutical items that go

stories.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing appointed president after ‘sham’ election
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing seized control five years ago and plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos Min Aung Hlaing, the military general who plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos when he took power in the 2021 coup has been appointed president, months after widely condemned sham elections. Min Aung Hlaing, who is wanted by the prosecutor of the international criminal court for crimes against humanity against the Rohingya Muslim minority, was voted president by lawmakers on Friday. Mya

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump calls for more time to open strait of Hormuz; Iran says it shot down US fighter jet
stories.summary: Iranian attacks reported across the Gulf as Trump tells Tehran the US will launch further strikes Authorities in Abu Dhabi have reported two incidents of debris falling from intercepted aerial threats in the UAE capital, with one sparking a fire at a gas facility, The official Abu Dhabi Media Office said authorities responded to an incident of falling debris at the Habshan gas facilities. “Operations have been suspended while authorities respond to a fire,” it said in a post on X, adding that no

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.summary: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Israel’s PM says 70% of Iran's steel production capacity destroyed, hindering weapons production
stories.summary: Friday began with Israel saying it was under attack by a new barrage of missiles from Iran, as President Donald Trump warned the United States had yet to begin "destroying what's left" of the Islamic republic's infrastructure.

stories.title: Australia says it won’t raise drug prices after Trump’s 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals imported into US
stories.summary: Health minister Mark Butler says federal government is ‘not negotiating’ when it comes to removing price protections on common medications Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia will not cave in to pressure from pharmaceutical giants and the Trump administration by removing consumer price protections on common medications, the health minister, Mark Butler says. Donald Trump imposed a new 100% tariff on branded pharmaceuticals imported into the US overnight, Austra

stories.title: Kuwait’s military says its air defences working to intercept missiles – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. You can read about the latest major development in the conflict here . Trump has claimed that Iran was “right at the doorstep” of gaining a nuclear weapon . Earlier on Wednesday the president said he did not care about Iran’s stock of highly enriched uranium (HEU), arguing it was deep underground and could be monitored by satellite. From the very beginning my campaign for president in 2015, I said I would never allow Iran to have a nuclear weapon. This regime has been ch

stories.title: Canadian woman held with daughter by ICE is released after nearly three weeks of detention
stories.summary: Tania Warner is fitted with ankle monitor and released along with seven-year-old daughter Ayla Luca after being deemed not a flight risk A Canadian woman and her seven-year-old daughter, who were held for nearly three weeks in a notorious detention center by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), were released on Thursday evening after posting a bond of $9,500. Tania Warner and her daughter Ayla Luca, originally from British Columbia, are both Canadian citizens. Warner moved to the US in

stories.title: ‘Every night they are bombarding’: at border crossing, some Iranians are fleeing war and some are heading home
stories.summary: People leaving Iran for Turkey tell of impact of bombs and internet blackouts, while others are travelling the other way to be closer to relatives in peril He could not help but splutter out a laugh at the question. Amir, whose name has been changed for his safety, had just crossed the Kapıköy border point in eastern Turkey, a mountain pass between snow-topped peaks that is one of the few gateways to the west from Iran. Until a few weeks ago, this was a busy place, popular among Iranian daytripp

stories.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
stories.summary: Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’ People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster. Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

stories.title: US judge rules against deporting man whose murder conviction was overturned after 44 years in prison
stories.summary: Decision came after hearing in which Subramanyam Vedam, 64, said he didn’t kill Thomas Kinser when he was 19 A judge has cleared the way for the potential release of an Indian citizen who was taken into Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody last year after his Pennsylvania murder conviction was overturned following four decades in prison. The decision came the day after the four-hour hearing in which Subramanyam Vedam insisted he did not fatally shoot Thomas Kinser in 1980 and was qu

stories.title: Cuba to pardon more than 2,000 prisoners as US eases fuel blockade
stories.summary: Havana calls releases a Holy Week humanitarian gesture as Russian tanker is allowed to reach oil-starved island Cuba has announced plans to pardon 2,010 prisoners as a “humanitarian” gesture during Holy Week, amid heightened US pressure on the government in Havana. The announcement on Thursday came days after Donald Trump eased a de facto oil blockade of Cuba by allowing a Russian tanker to deliver crude oil to the nation. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem only two cabinet members to be fired despite string of scandals facing male officials. Donald Trump has been accused of running a “misogynistic administration” after Pam Bondi became the second woman to be fired from a cabinet already dominated by men. Who will replace Bondi? stories.title: Young people ‘more likely to leave for health reasons when in low-paid, insecure jobs’
stories.summary: Research for TUC analyses link between job quality and economic inactivity, as UK youth unemployment rises Young people in the UK are more likely to leave their job for health reasons and become economically inactive when they work in insecure, low-paid sectors, a study has found. Research carried out for the Trades Union Congress by the consultancy Timewise charts a connection between the jobs young people are most likely to do – in hospitality, retail and care, for example – and the proportion

stories.title: Iranian tourist ban doesn’t align with the ‘Australian values’ Hedieh signed up to as a citizen
stories.summary: Visa ban makes Iranian-Australian feel her adopted country is a ‘home that doesn’t support you’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hedieh Jamshidian feared the window to see her mother, living in Tehran under waves of airstrikes, was closing. The Australian government had just announced it could block some visa holders from entering the country. So, Jamshidian, a 32-year-old Iranian Australian, decided to act quickly. Within a week she bought her mother, who held a thre

stories.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
stories.summary: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuter

stories.title: Iran war: UNSC to vote on Gulf-led resolution to open Hormuz
stories.summary: The UN Security Council will vote on a Bahraini proposal on the use of "defensive means" to open the Strait of Hormuz. Donald Trump has warned the US will target more Iranian bridges and "then electric power plants."

stories.title: Watch: Strike hits major Iranian bridge near Tehran, killing at least 8 civilians
stories.summary: Iran said strikes on the unfinished B1 bridge in Alborz province killed at least eight people and wounded 95 on Thursday, after families gathered near the site for Nature Day celebrations.

stories.title: Tehran fires at Israel as Trump warns US has not started 'destroying what is left' in Iran
stories.summary: Israel's military reported a new missile salvo from Iran on Friday, with its air defences operating to down them, but there were no immediate reports of casualties.

stories.title: Cuba to release more than 2,000 prisoners, as pressure from US mounts
stories.summary: US President Trump has made clear his desire to change Cuba's leadership and has placed the country under an oil embargo.

stories.title: Kyiv region under 'massive' daytime drone and missile attack by Russia, officials say
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv was ready for a truce over the Easter holidays, but the Kremlin said it had not received any proposals.

stories.title: UK deploys Rapid Sentry anti-drone system to Kuwait to fend off attacks, air force says
stories.summary: Gulf countries including Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain have been pulled into the Iran war, targeted by Iranian strikes in retaliation to attacks launched by the United States and Israel on 28 February.

stories.title: Pete Hegseth asks US Army's top general to step down
stories.summary: Gen Randy George was nominated for the role in 2023 by former President Joe Biden.

stories.title: ‘Nothing like the Louvre’: Italian art museum hit by cyberattack
stories.summary: Italy’s top art museum has been hacked potentially leaving its security systems vulnerable to future robberies. A cyberattack targeted Florence’s Uffizi Galleries earlier this year, the museum confirmed Friday, as Italian media alleged the breach forced authorities to move parts of its famed collection to secure vaults. But the Uffizi stressed in a note shared with POLITICO that the incident was “nothing like the Louvre,” which was recently forced to close parts of its operations following

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: Two NSW teenagers charged over violent extremist material allegedly linked to terror
stories.summary: Police say they found a ballistic-style vest, several notebooks and literature they allege contained extremist references Two New South Wales teenagers have been charged with allegedly possessing violent extremist material after police searched one of their homes and found a ballistic-style vest, several notebooks and literature they allege contained extremist references. The Australian federal police and NSW police released a statement on Friday morning confirming the boys, aged 15 and 16 and b

stories.title: Calm weather forecast for most of Australia over Easter weekend while BoM watches for possible cyclone
stories.summary: Bureau of Meteorology says a blend of fine days and showers expected for most capital cities over the coming days Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Many Australians can expect mild temperatures and a chance of showers over the Easter weekend, while meteorologists predict a cyclone will form in the Coral Sea from Saturday evening. stories.title: A day in the life of Asia’s fuel crisis
stories.summary: From farms in New Zealand to factories in Delhi, the effects of the oil crisis triggered by the Iran war are rippling across Asia Continue reading...

stories.title: Nuns who broke back into their Austrian convent ‘are step closer to being able to stay’
stories.summary: Plan for three nuns who escaped from care home last year to go to Rome thought to be positive sign of Vatican’s decision Three nuns who escaped from a care home to return to their convent in a castle close to Salzburg where they had spent most of their lives are a step closer to being able to stay there, sources close to them say. Sisters Bernadette, Regina and Rita, who are in their early to late eighties, broke into their convent home in Elsbethen last September with the help of former pupils

stories.title: Stop brunch! How a rustic Catalan meal is taking the fight to bland food and overtourism | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: As Barcelona groans under a surfeit of generic cafes, a grassroots movement is reviving the traditional ‘fork breakfast’. Anyone for pigs’ trotters? There are many worse ways to start your day than with eggs royale. The contrast in textures between a soft poached egg and a coarse, toasted English muffin is a thing of beauty, and the combination of smoked salmon and a lemony hollandaise sauce ties it together perfectly. The term “brunch” was coined in an essay in Hunter’s Weekly in 1895, and whil

stories.title: Storm Erminio lashes much of Greece as Saharan dust blankets Crete
stories.summary: South-east Italy also affected by heavy rain, and snow at higher altitudes, while deadly flooding hits Afghanistan Parts of the Mediterranean have been lashed by Storm Erminio this week. Heavy rain, thunderstorms and occasional bursts of hail affected much of Greece throughout Wednesday and Thursday, with the most severe conditions across south-eastern parts of the mainland and several islands in the southern Aegean Sea, including Crete, with streets flooded and vehicles stranded. Some of the he

stories.title: Trump claims Starmer is weak as he mocks PM and UK aircraft carriers
stories.summary: Footage shows US president saying UK ‘should be our best’ ally and accusing PM of prevarication over sending ships Middle East crisis – live updates Footage has emerged of Donald Trump mocking Keir Starmer by claiming the prime minister said he would have to consult his team before deciding whether to send UK aircraft carriers to the Middle East. In a new low for UK-US relations, Trump appeared to impersonate Starmer during an Easter lunch speech at the White House. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘It has been traumatic’: the Cornwall landmark left battered by Storm Goretti
stories.summary: St Michael’s Mount and the people who live near it are still healing from the scars left by storm’s 100mph winds Three months after Storm Goretti battered St Michael’s Mount in Cornwall , the signs of the storm’s power are still evident in the scars left by uprooted trees, piles of logs and the shaking of heads from islanders who have lived there for decades and never seen the like. “It really was something,” said Jack Beesley, a senior gardener. “We were shocked the morning after when we saw wh

stories.title: Iraqi leaders face balancing act as Iran conflict exposes deep rifts
stories.summary: Country is torn between those who hope for end to Tehran’s influence and those loyal to Islamic republic Of all the countries being pulled into the US-Israeli war on Iran, it is Iraq – a country that still bears the emotional and physical scars of the last time the Americans tried to reshape the region by force – where the conflict has exposed some of the deepest rifts. The war is dividing those who see the attacks on Iran as a way to end Tehran’s longstanding influence over Iraqi politics from

stories.title: It can’t be them: London national lottery jackpot winner misses out on £10.6m
stories.summary: Ticket bought in Bexley expires after 180-day claim period, despite extensive search for holder For many people in south-east London, the thought of winning the lottery and becoming an overnight millionaire would be a wonderful dream. For one person, however, that dream is now a nightmare after missing the deadline to claim a jackpot of more than £10m. National lottery players have 180 days to claim their winnings, but are entitled to nothing after the deadline has passed. Despite an “extensive

stories.title: ‘This is what you don’t see in F1’: the university where future race engineers are made
stories.summary: With alumni in every F1 team, Oxford Brookes University’s Formula Student team is the most prestigious in the country At the Oxford Brookes Headington campus, more than 100 students are busy building the fastest, best designed race car possible for this year’s Formula Student competition. Oxford Brookes Racing (OBR) is the UK’s most prestigious Formula Student team. They’ve won more design awards than any UK university, and frequently occupy the international race’s top spots. Continue reading..

stories.title: Todd Blanche takes over justice department after Pam Bondi sacking – US politics live
stories.summary: Trump announced deputy attorney general, who was his former personal attorney, would act up until permanent replacement is confirmed Trump accused of running ‘misogynistic administration’ after Bondi dismissal Sign up for the Breaking News US email The US labor market picked up in March as employers showed signs of resilience amid the US-Israel war in Iran . After an extraordinary contraction in February , employers added 178,000 jobs last month , ahead of economists’ expectations of about 70,00

stories.title: ‘You have to be serious’: Macron criticises Trump’s mixed messages about Nato and Iran
stories.summary: Speaking in South Korea, the French president defended the transatlantic alliance and called for return to peace Middle East crisis – live updates Emmanuel Macron has sharply criticised Donald Trump’s inconsistent and often contradictory pronouncements on the Iran war and Nato, saying if “you want to be serious” it was better not to come out with something different every day. “There is too much talk … and it’s all over the place,” the French president said on Thursday during a state visit to So

stories.title: Alleged maple syrup scam in Quebec uncovered by Canadian broadcaster
stories.summary: A Radio-Canada reporter noticed his maple syrup tasted odd; testing revealed it was adulterated with cane sugar An investigation by Canada ’s national broadcaster has found that a major Quebec producer has been diluting its maple syrup with cane sugar and selling the fraudulent product to grocery chains. In a sting operation that involved false identities and covert recordings, journalists from Radio-Canada’s Enquête programme found that a low-cost syrup sold in major grocery store chains was he

stories.title: Lebanon hit by fresh Israeli raid and Hezbollah response
stories.summary: Deadly Israeli strikes in Lebanon hit a building in Zebdine, near Nabatieh. The raid came as Hezbollah launched rockets and drones across the border and regional tensions deepened.

stories.title: Artemis II astronauts leave Earth's orbit, rocket towards Moon
stories.summary: Artemis II astronauts have officially begun their journey to the Moon after conducting a successful thruster firing. Humans are set to fly around the Moon for the first time in over 50 years.

stories.title: Are Iran-allied paramilitaries dragging Iraq into the war?
stories.summary: After a large convoy of Iraqis crossed into Iran this week, observers worried they would join Iran's war effort against the US and Israel. But, experts say, Iraq's situation is way more complicated than that.

stories.title: Germany news: Economist suggests autobahn speed limit amid Iran-enduced fuel price spike
stories.summary: A leading economist has recommended a temporary highway speed limit due to rising fuel costs amid the Iran war. Meanwhile, protesters are gearing up for annual Easter peace marches. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Germany: Easter peace marches planned in shadow of war
stories.summary: Germany's peace movement is gearing up for its traditional Easter peace marches, with the war in the Middle East and the reintroduction of conscription causing anxiety among Germans.

stories.title: India news: Modi, Gandhi invoke Jesus Christ's teachings on Good Friday
stories.summary: Indian leaders marked Good Friday with messages of reflection, compassion and unity. Meanwhile, an Iranian crude oil tanker bound for India has diverted to China. DW has more.

stories.title: Myanmar coup leader Min Aung Hlaing elected president
stories.summary: Myanmar's parliament has elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as president of the Southeast Asian country. He has effectively led Myanmar since the 2021 coup that ousted the democratic government led by Aung San Suu Kyi.

stories.title: Ukraine: Kharkiv under repeated attack on 1,500th day of war
stories.summary: Ukrainian authorities say Russia is deploying "new tactics" as the city of Kharkiv comes under rolling aerial assaults from missiles and drones fitted with jet engines. Six people have been killed across Ukraine.

stories.title: Cuba: 2,000 prisoners pardoned in Holy Week gesture
stories.summary: The move, which Cuba has described as a "humanitarian" gesture, comes after the United States allowed a Russian oil tanker to deliver crude to the island. But tensions between Havana and Washington persist.

stories.title: Inside India's courts, AI's growing role sparks concern
stories.summary: The increasing use of artificial intelligence in courtrooms raises worries that the technology may aggravate bias and structural distortions in the legal system.

stories.title: Burkina Faso: Army behind most civilian deaths — report
stories.summary: Human Rights Watch says Burkina Faso's security forces and allied militias "appear to be more brutal and violent" than militant groups. The Fulani ethnic group has reportedly been one of the targets.

stories.title: Macron dismisses Trump's 'inelegant' dig about his marriage
stories.summary: Trump took a dig at Macron while lambasting NATO, saying the French president's wife "treats him extremely badly." Macron rejected the notion that the Middle East conflict be treated as a "show."

stories.title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth asks US Army chief to step down as Iran war grinds on
stories.summary: Randy George 'will be retiring from his position as the 41st Chief of Staff of the Army effective immediately,' said Sean Parnell, the Pentagon’s top spokesman. George has held the post since August 2023 under the Biden administration.

stories.title: Researchers spent years interviewing 160 Bigfoot hunters - this is what they learnt
stories.summary: There have been thousands of reported Bigfoot sightings since it rose to prominence in the 1960s.

stories.title: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president
stories.summary: General Min Aung Hlaing has been chosen as the next president by the newly-elected parliament.

stories.title: Watch: Artemis II astronauts speak from space
stories.summary: The crew on board the Artemis II spoke by video link, sharing messages for their families and experiences of the trip so far.

stories.title: Belgium turns Easter chocolate into giant edible sculpture
stories.summary: Belgium is celebrating Easter with giant edible sculptures as nearly 40 chocolatiers from across the country present their creations in Brussels for the fourth Bel’Oeuf exhibition.

stories.title: Ukraine’s ancient Easter egg art helps preserve national identity
stories.summary: Ukraine is preparing for Easter with the pysanka, a hand‑decorated egg whose colours and symbols differ from one region to another and carry both religious and historical meaning.

stories.title: French ship crosses Strait of Hormuz in first Western European transit during Iran war
stories.summary: A vessel owned by France’s CMA CGM has become the first ship tied to Western Europe to cross the Strait of Hormuz since the outbreak of the Iran war in late February, according to ship tracking data.

stories.title: Three members of Greek government quit over EU farm subsidy scandal, state TV says
stories.summary: Most of the fraudulent subsidies went to the island of Crete, where the family of Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis has been politically influential for over a century.

stories.title: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
stories.summary: Capt Ibrahim Traoré, who seized power in 2023, says democratic rule would not work for his country.

stories.title: Artemis II leaves Earth's orbit on track for far side of the Moon
stories.summary: The mission's last, big push on its lunar journey takes humans out of the Earth's orbit for the first time since 1972.

stories.title: Putin issues ultimatum to Armenia over closer ties with EU
stories.summary: In a rare blunt exchange at the Kremlin, Russia's president issued a public warning to Armenia, telling Yerevan to choose between the EU and Russia. The Armenian prime minister responded that in his country, the people of Armenia run a democratic political process.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.summary: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ukrainian children taken at gunpoint by Russian forces in Kherson, documents show
stories.summary: Euronews obtained a copy of one of the documented episodes of Russia's systemic abduction and forceful deportation of Ukrainian children from temporarily occupied territories.

stories.title: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
stories.summary: Bondi's time as top US law enforcement officer was overshadowed by her handling of the Epstein files.

stories.title: Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US Army?
stories.summary: He will replace Randy George, who was asked to step down from his post by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.summary: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: France's Muslim gathering ban overturned by courts
stories.summary: The Paris police department had argued that the four-day gathering was a security threat because it could be a target of terrorism.

stories.title: Albania on alert after US warning as Bulgaria confirms Iran diplomatic note
stories.summary: Albania is on alert after the United States Embassy in Tirana warned of possible threats linked to the Tehran regime. Meanwhile, Bulgaria confirmed it received a warning from Iran over US military use of its airports.

stories.title: Azerbaijan-European Union Relations: Current State and Prospects
stories.summary: Whether in politics, the economy or energy security, relations between Azerbaijan and the EU have been developing dynamically, writes Dr. Matin Mammadli in an opinion piece for Euronews. Increased investment, technology transfer and visa facilitation would further strengthen these ties.

stories.title: Israel’s military stretched thin by regional wars and settler violence
stories.summary: Amid reports of troop deployment to the West Bank to contain settler violence and warnings of manpower shortage by Israel's top general, Euronews spoke to two Israeli experts to gauge the pressure on the country's security and internal stability.

stories.title: French MEP Rima Hassan in French police custody over alleged apology for terrorism
stories.summary: Hassan, who was born stateless in a refugee camp for Palestinians in Syria, is one of France's most vocal pro-Palestine activists and has already had brushes with the law for perceived glorification of terrorism.

stories.title: What to know about Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche
stories.summary: The former prosecutor has a long-standing relationship with President Trump, having represented him in the criminal hush-money case.

stories.title: Trump undermining NATO by creating doubt about US commitment, Macron says
stories.summary: The US president has repeatedly railed against Western partners for not responding to his call to assemble a naval force to help reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz, which has effectively been closed by Tehran.

stories.title: Orange skies over Crete as Storm Erminio sweeps Saharan sand across Greek island – in pictures
stories.summary: Storm Erminio has caused widespread destruction in parts of Greece, including the Attica region where a man died in Nea Makri. On the island of Crete, skies turned an eerie orange as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from North Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump’s surreal speech on Iran shed no light on his goals | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: The US president couldn’t give a single coherent reason for why this aggressive war of choice must still be prosecuted Donald Trump’s self-congratulatory speech on Iran on Wednesday night was as puzzling as it was divorced from reality. I had hoped he would declare victory and end the war. Some feared he might provide cover for a ground invasion. Instead, he told us in essence to be patient, that he is almost done, but he was utterly unclear about what more there is to accomplish. If there was e

stories.title: Iran war: How long can Tehran's asymmetric strategy hold?
stories.summary: After many years of sanctions and weeks of US-Israeli bombing, the Iranian regime is shaken — but still very much capable of threatening the region.

stories.title: Uganda: Four children stabbed dead at Kampala kindergarten
stories.summary: The suspected assailant was arrested at the scene and remains in police custody. His motive remains unclear.

stories.title: As Iran war rages, Europe gears up for energy crisis
stories.summary: The EU is waking up to an energy crisis and asking people to reduce energy consumption, as fears of a supply crunch set in. Energy analysts are calling for concrete measures and a boost in investments for renewables.

stories.title: Middle East war: Few solutions to fuel shortages in Africa
stories.summary: Africa is running low on fuel. The US-Israel-Iran war has sent fuel prices skyrocketing. Are African countries doing enough to calm the fuel market?

stories.title: Germany: Chancellor Merz has never been more unpopular
stories.summary: The CDU/CSU and SPD made many promises to voters. But according to polls, Germans are deeply disappointed with the governing coalition and have little hope that things will improve.

stories.title: Trump removes Attorney General Pam Bondi
stories.summary: Bondi has faced criticism in Washington over her handling of investigative files related to the late financier and sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.summary: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Severe snow and floods in Italy leave residents trapped and damage infrastructure
stories.summary: Heavy rain and snowstorms in central and southern Italy brought down bridges and left people trapped in their homes on Thursday. Firefighters used special snowcat trucks to plow through huge banks of snow in small towns in the Italian region of Molise.

stories.title: Iran vows 'crushing, more destructive' attacks on US and Israel after Trump threats
stories.summary: The comments come after a prime-time address at the White House in which US President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iran "back to the Stone Ages, where they belong."

stories.title: Over 1.6 million Ukrainians returned home to frontline areas despite Russian attacks, report shows
stories.summary: Feeling homesick outweighs the risk of living in frontline areas for over 1.6 million Ukrainians who have returned home despite constant Russian attacks. ‘Save the Children’ researchers stated that “going back to a warzone is never a choice anyone makes lightly.”

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.summary: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: German artist sentenced to prison by Russian court for carnival display mocking Putin
stories.summary: Jacques Tilly has been designing and building floats for Düsseldorf’s Carnival parade, one of Germany’s best-known, since 1984.

stories.title: Russia to send second oil tanker to Cuba as energy crisis continues, Moscow says
stories.summary: Cuba produces barely 40% of its required fuel and relies on imports to sustain its crumbling energy grid.

stories.title: Watch: Trump's shifting deadlines for ending Iran war
stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent analyses the US President's latest White House address.

stories.title: Trump sacks Attorney General Pam Bondi and replaces with ex-personal lawyer
stories.summary: Bondi has reportedly drawn Trump's ire by falling short with efforts to prosecute perceived opponents such as former FBI director James Comey and New York Attorney General Letitia James.

stories.title: Videos show explosions in central Iran as conflict with Israel intensifies
stories.summary: Iran was hit by a powerful new wave of strikes on Wednesday when massive explosions and thick plumes of smoke rose over Baharestan, in the Esfahan region of central Iran.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.summary: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: Why isn't Artemis II landing on the Moon?
stories.summary: The US is aiming to be the first country to land humans on the lunar surface again by 2028 - so what's this mission for?

stories.title: From the Chrism Mass to the washing of the feet: Pope Leo’s first Easter season begins
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV begins his first Easter as pontiff by washing priests’ feet, following a tradition previously used by Pope Francis, while warning against domination in a troubled world.

stories.title: Belarus passes bill to crack down on LGBTQ+ rights echoing similar law in Russia
stories.summary: Belarus decriminalised homosexuality in 1994 after the collapse of the Soviet Union, but it doesn’t recognise same-sex marriages and lacks protection for LGBTQ+ rights.

stories.title: Judge dismisses most of Blake Lively's claims in Justin Baldoni harassment suit
stories.summary: The judge left in place three allegations against Baldoni, meaning the civil trial will go ahead next month.

stories.title: Arlo Parks: 'I got out of my head and into my body'
stories.summary: The singer-songwriter found herself on the dancefloor. On a new album, she asks us to join her.

stories.title: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin egy szavazatvásárlós hangfelvétel miatt mutogat a Fideszre, a párt szerint ez nem az ő stílusuk
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt dunaújvárosi jelöltje mindenesetre feljelentést tesz.

stories.title: A legfontosabb információk a húsvéti nyitvatartásról, közlekedési változásokról, parkolásról
stories.summary: Boltok, gyógyszertárak, MÁV, BKK, posta, áruházak: mikor mi lesz nyitva?

stories.title: Őrszemekkel, rontott szavazólapokkal és virtuális X-szel harcolnának a kizsarolt szavazatok ellen
stories.summary: Hogyan lehet megakadályozni, vagy legalább leleplezni azokat a módszeres csalásokat, amelyekkel mélyszegénységben élők szavazatait vásárolják meg? Civil koordinátorokkal és egy választási jogásszal jártuk körbe az eszközöket a nagyobb médiajelenléttől a Nemzeti Választási Bizottságnak benyújtott panaszokig.

stories.title: Nem is igazi pécsi, aki nem azt mondja, hogy Pécsett. Vagy mégis?
stories.summary: A Telex pécsi kötődésű munkatársaival és az ELTE egyetemi tanárával eredtünk a tudományos igazság nyomába.

stories.title: Kaposvári püspök: Azt a szót, hogy migráns, le se írhatom, mert azonnal ugranak a szóra és rám is
stories.summary: Szerinte ha elfogadja az ember, így az egyház tagjai is az ellenségkép-gyártást, akkor nem az evangéliumot szolgálják, hanem „a minket körülvevő propaganda jár át minket”.

stories.title: A Szahara homokját felkapva festette vörösre Krétát a hatalmas vihar
stories.summary: Az Erminio vihar áradásokat is okozott Görögország egyes részein, több járatot töröltek.

stories.title: Donald Trump a hidak lebombázásával fenyegeti Iránt
stories.summary: Közvetítésünk a közel-keleti háború 35. napjáról.

stories.title: Vasárnap már többórás napsütés is lehet, 20 fok fölé melegedhet a levegő
stories.summary: Hétfőn hidegfront érkezik, addig viszonylag kellemes tavasz lesz.

stories.title: Ezekért érdemes moziba menni április 3-tól április 10-ig
stories.summary: Itt a következő hét napra szóló Telex Diszpó.

stories.title: Erős változás a NER-gazdaság működésben: elkezdték kibeszélni egymást
stories.summary: Hogyan hathat Pálinkás Szilveszter százados videója a kampányra? Miért zuhant a kormányközeli cégek tőzsdei értéke? Elemzés az elmúlt napokról Bíró-Nagy Andrással és Brückner Gergellyel.

stories.title: Azt hiszi, elég volt már az esőből? Hát nagyon nem!
stories.summary: Múlt héten szépen eláztatta hazánkat a Deborah ciklon, de az ország nagy részén csak a felszínközeli rétegek nedvesedtek át, amik épphogy csökkentették a tavaszi aszály szintjét. Európa középső részén szintén sok esőre lenne szükség, de globálisan is nagy hiány mutatkozik a vízből.

stories.title: Újra elindultak a kamuprofilokról indított, fideszes üzeneteket sulykoló Facebook-hirdetések
stories.summary: Napi milliós összeget költ rá valaki, a hirdetések eddig másfél milliószor jelentek meg. A Meta nagyjából a felét lőtte le politikai tartalom miatt.

stories.title: Feljelentést tett az NVI, miután több száz romát próbáltak regisztrálni a nemzetiségi névjegyzékbe
stories.summary: A Népszava szerint több mint négyszáz roma adataival próbálhattak visszaélni a XVIII. kerületben.

stories.title: A reptértől Zugligetig a fél várost lezárják J. D. Vance érkezése miatt
stories.summary: Az útvonal alapján Vance elmegy a Karmelitába és a Parlamentbe is, majd az amerikai nagykövetségi rezidencián száll meg.

stories.title: Ukrán feliratos, kamu választási plakátok jelentek meg Debrecenben, a Tisza kifogást emelt
stories.summary: A jelölt és a párt neve, illetve a „szavazz” felirat is ukránul szerepelt rajta.

stories.title: Letelt a 72 óra, visszalépett a jelöltségtől Tordai Bence
stories.summary: A képviselő három napja jelentette be, hogy amennyiben a Tisza Párt jelöltje nem fordul vissza, úgy ő fog.

stories.title: Bíró-Nagy András: Elkezdett kopni, aztán a kampány legintenzívebb szakaszában besült a Fidesz legfőbb kampányfegyvere
stories.summary: A 2026 élő műsorának vendége a Policy Solutions vezetője volt, aki beszélt Pálinkás Szilveszter százados interjújának hatásairól, a Fidesz kampányának hibáiról és arról, hogy már az egész társadalomban érezhető egy fordulat a választási kampány hajrájában.

stories.title: Visszalép a Jobbik jelöltje Pest 12. választókörzetében
stories.summary: Szerinte a rendszerváltás érdeke előrébb való, mint egyéni ambíciói.

stories.title: Elhagyta a Föld körüli pályát az Artemis II., megtette utolsó nagy lépését a Hold felé
stories.summary: A NASA szerint hiba nélkül ment végig a főhajtómű beindítása.

stories.title: Vonaton robbantott petárdákat egy német férfi, tizenketten megsérültek
stories.summary: A húszéves férfit az utasok a WC-be zárták. A támadásnak politikai motivációja lehetett.

stories.title: Fájó szívvel, de távozik az olasz labdarúgó-válogatott szövetségi kapitánya
stories.summary: Rino Gattuso közös megegyezéssel hagyja ott posztját, miután az olaszok sorozatban harmadszor maradtak le a világbajnokságról.

stories.title: Trump lashes out at Nato: will Europe stand up to him? - The Latest
stories.summary: Donald Trump has said he is considering pulling the US out of Nato, likening the alliance to a ‘paper tiger’. It comes after weeks of denouncements from the US president against allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. When asked about Trump’s comments, Keir Starmer said: ‘Nato is the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen’ and ‘whatever the noise, I’m going to act in the British national interest in all the decisions that I make’. Lucy Hough speaks to the

stories.title: ‘System malfunction’ causes robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China
stories.summary: Distressed riders who were stranded for hours say Apollo Go customer service agents offered ‘useless platitudes’ A “system malfunction” has caused several self-driving robotaxis to stall in the middle of the road in China, police have confirmed, after distressed riders were stranded for hours. Local authorities in the central Chinese city of Wuhan said they began receiving calls “one after another” on Tuesday night from riders reporting that autonomous vehicles operated by the Chinese internet c

stories.title: Switzerland eyes dropping purchase of US Patriot air defence system over delivery delays
stories.summary: Switzerland, which is not in NATO, had ordered five Patriot systems in 2022, with delivery scheduled to begin this year and to be completed in 2028.

stories.title: Malawi’s Chakwera warns: Fighting corruption can turn against you - Interview
stories.summary: “The fight you engage in fights back," said the former president as he reflected on his time in power, corruption allegations and governance reform in Africa.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez
stories.summary: Washington has lifted sanctions on Rodríguez, boosting her legitimacy as a US-recognised leader and opening doors for closer oil and business ties.

stories.title: Videos show explosions after a series of strikes hits Isfahan in central Iran
stories.summary: Massive explosions and thick plumes of smoke rose in Isfahan after a powerful wave of strikes hit central Iran on Wednesday. The attacks follow earlier US strikes in the same region that triggered a large fireball. Tehran retaliates with strikes on Gulf facilities and missile launches toward Israel.

stories.title: Germany: Conservatives link immigration with crime
stories.summary: Will German citizens feel safer if more immigrants are deported? Chancellor Friedrich Merz has been giving that impression. But the reality is more complex.

stories.title: Reeves criticises Trump for starting Iran war with no ‘clear plan’ to get out of it – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Starmer says he understands why people are concerned about the cost of living. He says he has already set out a five-point plan to deal with the crisis. Just look at what’s happening today. Today your energy bills will be cut because of the action that we took at the budget. And whatever happens in Iran, that price is now fixed until July. The most effective way we can support the cost of living in Britain is to push for de-escalation in the Middle East, and a reopening o

stories.title: New Zealand signs defence pact with Cook Islands after quarrel over China deal
stories.summary: Agreement comes after Wellington halted millions in aid to its former colony after Cook Islands formed strategic partnership with Beijing New Zealand and the Cook Islands have signed a defence and security declaration, ending a year-long diplomatic row that erupted after the Cook Islands struck strategic agreements with China. The Cook Islands was a dependent New Zealand colony from 1901-65 but has since operated as a self-governing nation in “free association” with New Zealand. Its roughly 17,0

stories.title: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warning One person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves. The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an island in Indonesia’s North Maluku province. Co

stories.title: Coordinated conflict: how the Ukraine and Iran wars are starting to overlap
stories.summary: Battlefield outcomes are connected by the sharing of weapons and intelligence as well as the damage to the global economy The Iran and Ukraine wars are becoming more intertwined with every passing week – to the point that some analysts argue the two conflicts are beginning to merge. Quite how each war will affect the trajectory of the other is hard to predict, but it is already clear that their interconnectedness is drawing more countries into both cauldrons, extending an arc of instability that

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president Delcy Rodríguez
stories.summary: US moves towards reestablishing working relations between two countries after abducting President Nicolás Maduro The US has lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting president, Delcy Rodríguez , in the latest step towards normalising relations between the two countries after US forces abducted her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro, and his wife. The couple were taken to New York after their abduction in January to face charges of alleged drug trafficking, to which both have pleaded not guilty. Continue r

stories.title: 41 polgármester aláírásával kérik a lakosokat Szabolcs-Szatmár-Bereg megyében, hogy szavazzanak a Fidesz jelöltjére
stories.summary: Korábban már Mórahalom fideszes polgármestere is levélben buzdította a helyieket, hogy szavazzanak a Fidesz helyi jelöltjére.

stories.title: Rákbeteg Rubint Réka
stories.summary: A fitneszedző négy évvel ezelőtt, 2022-ben értesült a betegségéről.

stories.title: Újabb miniszterét cserélte le Trump, Pam Bondi is lapátra került
stories.summary: Zsinórban a kabinet második női tárcavezetője távozik, ezúttal az igazságügyi minisztérium éléről.

stories.title: Szoboszlai a világ legértékesebb olyan játékosa, aki kihagyja a foci vb-t
stories.summary: Olyan nagy nevek hagyják ki az észak-amerikai vb-t, mint Lewandowski, Donnarumma vagy Osimhen.

stories.title: Lázár János felvetette, hogy az ukránok talán eladták az Amerikától és az EU-tól kapott fegyvereket az oroszoknak
stories.summary: „Mégiscsak Ukrajnáról van szó” – támasztotta alá a teóriát Szarvason a miniszter.

stories.title: Teljes kezességet nyújt az állam az olajkészletező szövetség hiteleire
stories.summary: Az MSZKSZ-nek várhatóan hitelre lesz szüksége ahhoz, hogy visszatöltse a Barátság-vezeték kiesése miatti olajkészleteket.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
stories.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

stories.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
stories.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

stories.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
stories.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

stories.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
stories.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

stories.title: 'Drill baby drill': Trump opens wilderness to big energy
stories.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the Trump administration pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

stories.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
stories.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

stories.title: Denmark: Warship sunk by Nelson's British fleet discovered
stories.summary: Over 200 years after being sunk by the British Royal Navy under Admiral Horatio Nelson, one of Denmark's most famous warships has been discovered at the bottom of Copenhagen Harbor — complete with some remarkable items.

stories.title: US Supreme Court casts doubt on Donald Trump's bid to limit birthright citizenship
stories.summary: Trump signed an executive order on his return to the White House last year decreeing that children born to parents in the United States illegally or on temporary visas would not automatically become US citizens

stories.title: Iran launches biggest salvo of missiles in three weeks at Israel, military officials say
stories.summary: Iranian missile fire on Israel has slowed in the past two weeks to around 10-15 missiles a day, down from around 90 on the first day of the war.

stories.title: Watch the moment Artemis II blasts into space on historic mission
stories.summary: After delays and technical issues, the first crewed Moon mission in 50 years finally took off from Florida and is now in Earth's orbit.

stories.title: Trump says Iran 'objectives are nearing completion' in first prime-time speech in defence of war
stories.summary: Speaking in his first national address since the war began on 28 February, Trump sought to reassure war-weary Americans that the offensive was worth the effort.

stories.title: Watch: NASA's Artemis II rocket launches on historic Moon mission
stories.summary: A NASA rocket carrying four astronauts blasted off from Florida, marking the first crewed lunar mission in more than 50 years.

stories.title: Russia launches massive drone barrage on Ukraine as peace talks stall
stories.summary: Diplomatic efforts to end Russia's all-out invasion of Ukraine have stalled in recent months, in part because the United States has shifted its focus towards the Iran war.

stories.title: Powerful 7.4 quake hits Indonesia’s Molucca Sea, tsunami alert lifted
stories.summary: Indonesia was struck by a powerful 7.4‑magnitude earthquake early on 2 April in the Molucca Sea, off Ternate in North Maluku, killing at least one person and briefly triggering a tsunami warning.

stories.title: Untaxed wealth of the top 0.1% eclipses assets of the poorest half of the world
stories.summary: The world's ultra-rich have stashed around $2.84 trillion (€2.47tn) in untaxed offshore accounts, a figure that surpasses the combined wealth of the bottom 50% of the human population, according to a new report.

stories.title: Iran war disrupts global aid supply, threatening life-saving operations across Africa
stories.summary: The Iran war is disrupting fuel and shipping, choking life-saving operations and pushing up hunger and poverty across Africa, Aid group IRC has warned.

stories.title: Sahara dust turns skies red over Crete
stories.summary: Strong winds carried Sahara dust across the Mediterranean, turning skies over Crete a striking red and yellow. Scenes captured in Rethymno and Georgioupoli show the sun dimmed by sand, creating an eerie, almost Martian atmosphere.

stories.title: Europe and Africa look beyond aid toward a partnership of interest
stories.summary: For decades, Europe has been a key partner to Africa, providing aid development support and shaping governance frameworks, but critics say the model is out of step with a changing global reality.

stories.title: Armenia’s unique salt mine asthma clinic faces closure as funds dry up
stories.summary: Armenia is home to a rare underground clinic beneath Yerevan, where asthma and allergy patients descend 235 metres into the Avan salt mine to breathe mineral‑rich air in a setting unlike any conventional hospital.

stories.title: Sweden announces €800 million investment in air and drone defence systems
stories.summary: The investment would cover multiple systems, such as radar and anti-aircraft weapons systems as well as electronic warfare platforms.

stories.title: Russia fired record number of drones at Ukraine in March, new air force data show
stories.summary: Ukraine has sought to scale up its air defences in response, deploying cheap interceptor drones to destroy their Russian counterparts.

stories.title: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".
